docs.title: 👋 Welcome to AuraConnect Documentation
docs.text: This is the official documentation for the AuraConnect platform.

docs.title: 📚 Navigate the Docs
docs.text:
• 📅 Development Roadmap
  • 🧭 Phase 1: Discovery & Planning
  • 🎨 Phase 2: UI/UX Design
  • ⚙️ Phase 3: Backend Dev
  • 🤖 Phase 4: AI Agent Framework
  • 📱 Phase 5: Frontend Dev
  • 🔗 Phase 6: Integrations
  • 🧪 Phase 7: QA & Testing
  • 🧑‍🍳 Phase 8: Pilot Launch
  • 🚀 Phase 9: Public Launch

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: AuraConnect is composed of modular services that operate independently but interact over REST APIs and shared databases.

docs.title: Services
docs.text:
• Backend: Auth, Orders, Inventory, Payroll
  • Frontend: Admin dashboard, reports
  • Mobile: Manager interface, offline-ready

docs.title: DevOps
docs.text:
• Docker Compose for local dev
  • PostgreSQL for data persistence
  • GitHub Actions for CI/CD

docs.title: Dev Roadmap
docs.text: This section includes all development phases with sprint breakdowns and planning files.

docs.title: 🧮 Total Duration: 13–17 weeks (~3–4 months)
docs.text:
Start Date: June 12, 2025 Team: 1–2 Devs + 1 AI Engineer + 1 UI/UX Designer

Welcome to the full development plan for AuraConnect.

docs.title: 📅 Timeline Overview
docs.text: Phase Name Duration Link 1 Discovery & Planning 1–1.5 wks Read Phase 1 2 UI/UX & Wireframes 1.5–2 wks Read Phase 2 3 Core Backend Dev 2–3 wks Read Phase 3 4 AI Agent Framework 2–3 wks Read Phase 4 5 Frontend Development 3–4 wks Read Phase 5 6 Integrations 2 wks Read Phase 6 7 Internal QA & Testing 1–1.5 wks Read Phase 7 8 Restaurant Pilot Launch 2 wks Read Phase 8 9 Public/Beta Launch 1 wk Read Phase 9

docs.title: 🧭 Phase 1 – Sprint Plan: Discovery & Planning (7 Days)
🎯 Goal: Define clear scope, journeys, and system design
✅ Day-by-Day Breakdown
docs.text: Day Task Deliverable Day 1 Kickoff meeting, create Notion board & GitHub repo Workspace ready Day 2 Feature scoping – MVP vs Full product Feature matrix Day 3–4 Map user journeys (Customer, Staff, Agent, Admin) Journey diagrams Day 5 Low-fidelity wireframes (Customer App + Staff Dashboard) Sketches / Figma Day 6 Agent definition (LangChain roles, prompts, flows) Agent spec document Day 7 Architecture sketch – Backend, Frontend, Infra Tech stack + system diagram

docs.title: 🧾 Deliverables from Phase 1
docs.text:
• 📋 Feature Scope Matrix (MVP vs Full)
  • 🔄 Journey Flows (Customer, Staff, Agent)
  • 🧠 Agent Role Definition (LangChain / AutoGen prompt logic)
  • 🧰 Initial Wireframes (mobile & dashboard views)
  • 🏗️ System Architecture Diagram (FastAPI + DB + React Native)

docs.title: 💡 Notes
docs.text:
• A simplified MVP (Reservation system + Menu + AI Agent) can go live in 6–8 weeks
  • Full-stack modular features can be added post-launch
  • Staged releases are supported: Reservation-only → Order system → AI Dashboard

docs.title: 🧭 Phase 1 – Discovery & Planning
docs.text: Duration: 1–1.5 Weeks Goal: Define features, user journeys, wireframes, and AI behaviors.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• Project kickoff + Notion setup
  • Feature matrix (MVP vs Full)
  • User journey flows (Customer, Staff, Agent)
  • Wireframe sketches
  • Agent roles + system architecture outline

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Feature scope matrix
  • User journey flowcharts
  • Agent behavior documentation
  • Architecture & tech stack overview
  • Low-fidelity wireframes

docs.title: 📅 Duration: 1.5–2 Weeks
docs.text: Goal: Design all visual interfaces and interactive flows for the customer app, staff dashboard, and agent interfaces.

docs.title: 🗓️ Sprint Plan
docs.text: Day Task Deliverable Day 1 Define UX priorities (mobile-first, accessibility, speed) UX checklist Day 2 Customer app user flow (menu, reservations, orders) Flow diagram Day 3 Staff dashboard flow (orders, tables, chat) Flowcharts Day 4 AI agent interface (prompt feedback, UI layout) Sketches Day 5 Admin panel wireframes (optional) Low-fi wireframes Day 6–7 Build full wireframe set in Figma or tool Wireframe kit Day 8 Create style guide (colors, fonts, icons) Design system Day 9 Component library (mobile + web) Reusable components Day 10 UI handoff prep + dev feedback round Exported assets

docs.title: 📦 Deliverables
docs.text:
• ✅ Flow Diagrams (Customer, Staff, Agent)
  • ✅ Wireframes for all key screens
  • ✅ Style Guide (typography, colors, icons)
  • ✅ Component Library
  • ✅ Final Figma or design file (link/export)
  • ✅ PNG Wireframe Mockup

docs.title: 📁 Folder Structure (Recommended)
docs.text: /design ├── wireframes/ │ ├── customer_app.png │ ├── staff_dashboard.png │ ├── ai_agent_interface.png ├── styleguide.md ├── components/ │ ├── buttons.png │ ├── navigation.png └── figma_link.txt

docs.title: 📎 Attached Asset
docs.text: • A_wireframe_mockup_of_AuraConnect.png – Home screen wireframe preview

docs.title: 📅 Duration: 2–3 Weeks
docs.text: Goal: Build the complete backend foundation using FastAPI. Implement the database, APIs, and integrations.

docs.title: 🗓️ Sprint Plan
docs.text: Day(s) Task Deliverable Day 1–2 Set up FastAPI project structure + Docker Dev server ready Day 3–4 Define DB schema (Users, Tables, Menu, Orders) SQLAlchemy models Day 5–6 Implement menu endpoints GET /menu, GET /menu/{id} Day 7–8 Reservation API + conflict checking POST /reservations Day 9–10 Orders CRUD endpoints POST /orders, GET /orders/{id} Day 11–12 Staff roles + dashboard endpoints GET /staff/tasks, PUT /staff/assign Day 13–14 AI Event API + WebSocket hook /events/agent, WS /agent-live Day 15–16 Auth with JWT + onboarding POST /register, POST /login Day 17–18 Middleware: Logging, CORS, rate-limiting Middleware added Day 19–21 Testing + Swagger validation Unit tests + /docs complete

docs.title: 📦 Deliverables
docs.text:
• ✅ Dockerized FastAPI backend
  • ✅ REST API: Menu, Reservations, Orders, Staff
  • ✅ PostgreSQL schema + Alembic
  • ✅ Auth system (JWT)
  • ✅ WebSocket + AI Agent stub
  • ✅ Unit tests + Swagger docs
  • ✅ OpenAPI JSON file

docs.title: 📁 Suggested Project Layout
docs.text:
/backend
  ├── app/
  │   ├── main.py
  │   ├── api/
  │   │   ├── routes/
  │   │   └── deps.py
  │   ├── core/
  │   │   ├── config.py
  │   ├── db/
  │   │   ├── models.py
  │   │   └── base.py
  │   ├── schemas/
  │   ├── services/
  │   └── events/
  ├── tests/
  ├── alembic/
  └── Dockerfile

docs.title: 💡 Notes
docs.text:
• Use PostgreSQL (prod), SQLite (dev optional)
  • Design for future event-based integrations
  • Env support for Twilio, Firebase, Stripe

docs.title: 🤖 Phase 4 – AI Agent Framework
docs.text: Duration: 2–3 Weeks Goal: Integrate LangChain or AutoGen to build the customer AI agent.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• Setup LangChain / AutoGen agents
  • Create agent prompt logic + failover
  • Backend hooks for agent events
  • WebSocket / webhook flow
  • Agent logging and feedback

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Customer AI assistant
  • Prompt templates
  • Agent API & WebSocket hooks
  • Logs and fallback mechanism

docs.title: 📱 Phase 5 – Frontend Development
docs.text: Duration: 3–4 Weeks Goal: Build frontend in React Native (App) + React (Web Dashboard).

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• Navigation + routing setup
  • Customer menu, orders, reservations
  • Staff dashboard (orders, tables)
  • API integrations
  • Real-time socket connections

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Mobile app (iOS + Android-ready)
  • Web dashboard
  • Styled components
  • Live data bindings

docs.title: 🔗 Phase 6 – Integrations
docs.text: Duration: 2 Weeks Goal: Integrate Twilio, Firebase, Stripe, POS, and delivery APIs.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• Twilio/Firebase for notifications
  • Stripe/Razorpay for payments
  • POS API bridge (optional)
  • Delivery service webhook layer

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Modular service hooks
  • Testable API bridges
  • Secure key management

docs.title: 🧪 Phase 7 – Internal QA & Testing
docs.text: Duration: 1–1.5 Weeks Goal: Test the app across all user roles in real scenarios.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• Role-based testing (customer, staff, admin)
  • Manual QA scripts
  • Load and stress testing
  • Bug tracking + fixing

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• QA checklists
  • Test suite results
  • Bugfix logs

docs.title: 🧑‍🍳 Phase 8 – Restaurant Pilot Launch
docs.text: Duration: 2 Weeks Goal: Deploy to real restaurants and gather feedback.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• Live restaurant onboarding
  • Analytics integration
  • Staff feedback sessions
  • Agent tuning

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Pilot deployment logs
  • Feedback reports
  • Adjusted UX or agent behavior

docs.title: 🚀 Phase 9 – Public/Beta Launch
docs.text: Duration: 1 Week Goal: Launch cloud-hosted beta version with onboarding flows.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• Deploy to Railway/Render/AWS
  • Setup onboarding experience
  • Write documentation
  • Collect beta feedback

docs.title: Deliverables
docs.text:
• Cloud-hosted live version
  • User onboarding assets
  • Dev + user docs

docs.title: 1. 🤖 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
The AI Customization Suite empowers restaurants to automate, personalize, and optimize experiences using AI across operations, customer engagement, and insights.

Goals:

  • Enable dynamic menu recommendations and pricing
  • Automate customer replies, reviews, and FAQs
  • Provide analytics summaries and action suggestions
  • Support custom AI model integrations for enterprise clients

docs.title: 2. 🎯 Key AI Capabilities
docs.text:
• Menu suggestion engine based on trends, stock, and demand
  • Smart response engine (chat/FAQ/autoreplies)
  • AI-generated insights on sales, wastage, and performance
  • Custom prompts and workflows (e.g., chef-assist, shift-assist)

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services:

  • AICore – Hosts model logic and integrates APIs
  • PromptEngine – Template-based prompt manager
  • RecommendationService – Menu & pricing suggestions
  • InsightGenerator – Generates summaries, tips
  • ChatAgent – Handles customer interaction flows
[User/Staff Input] ─▶ [AICore] ─▶ [PromptEngine / Model API]
                             │
               ┌────────────┴─────────────┐
               ▼                          ▼
    [RecommendationService]      [InsightGenerator]
               │                          ▼
               ▼                    [Dashboard UI / Logs]
        [Dynamic Menu API]           [Summary Cards]

docs.title: Menu Optimization:
docs.text:
1. Inventory + sales data analyzed
  2. Suggest low-waste, high-margin items
  3. Dynamic pricing based on demand or time

docs.title: Customer Chatbot Flow:
docs.text:
1. Customer asks a question
  2. ChatAgent uses PromptEngine
  3. AI-generated response returned

docs.title: Performance Summary:
docs.text:
1. Manager opens report section
  2. InsightGenerator fetches and condenses KPIs
  3. “Smart Tips” displayed (e.g. "Reduce onion wastage by 15%")

docs.title: AI Services
docs.text:
• POST /ai/menu/suggest
  • POST /ai/chat/respond
  • GET /ai/insights/performance
  • POST /ai/custom/prompt

docs.title: Table: ai_prompts
docs.text: | id | name | context | template | last_used |

docs.title: Table: ai_logs
docs.text: | id | user_id | input | output | timestamp |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Code Stub
docs.text:
// ai.chat.service.ts
app.post("/ai/chat/respond", async (req, res) => {
  const { message } = req.body;
  const prompt = await buildPrompt("customer_reply", message);
  const reply = await openai.complete(prompt);
  res.json({ reply });
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Use OpenAI/Gemini API for LLM tasks; support local LLMs optionally
  • Prompts should be version-controlled for traceability
  • Rate limit AI usage per tenant if needed
  • Allow custom prompts per restaurant or role

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
The AI Customization Suite turns data and interactions into intelligent suggestions, automation, and customer satisfaction boosts. It adds a smart, extensible layer to every module in AuraConnect.

➡️ Optional module finale complete! Final module: Regulatory & Compliance Add-on

docs.title: 1. 📊 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module provides restaurant owners and managers with powerful insights into sales, operations, customer behavior, and team performance. It transforms raw data into actionable dashboards and exportable reports.

Goals:

  • Centralize KPIs from all core modules
  • Enable filtering, drill-downs, and trend analysis
  • Export reports (PDF, Excel)
  • Visualize real-time metrics and historical trends

docs.title: 2. 📋 Core Features
docs.text:
• Daily/weekly/monthly dashboards
  • Sales & revenue breakdowns
  • Staff performance analytics
  • Inventory wastage and cost reporting
  • Loyalty & customer behavior tracking

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services:

  • AnalyticsService – Data queries and aggregations
  • ReportingService – Exporting reports, scheduled delivery
  • DashboardEngine – Frontend data visualizations
  • DataWarehouse – Optimized tables for querying
  • AuthService – Scoped access to sensitive reports
[Dashboard UI] ─▶ [AnalyticsService] ─▶ [DataWarehouse]
                             │
                             ▼
                   [ReportingService] ─▶ [PDF/CSV Engine]

docs.title: Sales Dashboard Flow:
docs.text:
1. User opens dashboard
  2. Filters (date range, category) sent to API
  3. AnalyticsService queries warehouse
  4. Charts populate with result set

docs.title: Report Export Flow:
docs.text:
1. User clicks export/report schedule
  2. Report config saved (filters, format)
  3. Scheduled job generates and emails export

docs.title: Dashboards
docs.text:
• GET /analytics/sales
  • GET /analytics/inventory
  • GET /analytics/staff-performance

docs.title: Reports
docs.text:
• POST /report/export – on-demand PDF/CSV
  • POST /report/schedule – weekly/monthly
  • GET /report/history – logs and downloads

docs.title: 6. 🗃️ Data Schema (Warehouse Schema Design)
docs.text: Tables are denormalized for fast querying:

docs.title: Table: sales_agg
docs.text: | date | total_sales | avg_ticket | payment_types (jsonb) |

docs.title: Table: staff_metrics
docs.text: | date | staff_id | hours | orders_handled | tips |

docs.title: Table: inventory_agg
docs.text: | date | item_id | used_qty | waste | cost |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Code Stub
docs.text:
// analytics.service.ts
app.get("/analytics/sales", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  const { from, to } = req.query;
  const data = await db.query("SELECT * FROM sales_agg WHERE date BETWEEN $1 AND $2", [from, to]);
  res.json(data);
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Use materialized views or pre-computed tables for performance
  • PDF/CSV exports should support branded templates
  • Include access control to restrict financial data visibility
  • Enable embeddable widgets (e.g. Net Sales Today)

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
Analytics & Reporting is the brain of AuraConnect, turning raw operations into intelligence. This module enables strategic decision-making, performance tracking, and actionable insights across the platform.

➡️ Next up: Taxing & Payroll System

docs.title: 1. 🎯 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module manages customer data, engagement, and loyalty programs. It enables personalized experiences, reward incentives, and customer retention strategies through tiered memberships, referrals, and purchase behavior tracking.

Goals:

  • Centralize customer profiles and history
  • Reward recurring visits and high-value spenders
  • Enable referrals, coupons, and point systems
  • Segment customers for marketing campaigns

docs.title: 2. 👤 Core Features
docs.text:
• Customer profiles (name, contact, preferences)
  • Points-based loyalty system
  • Tiered membership levels (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold)
  • Referral tracking and incentives
  • Coupon/offer generation and validation

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services:

  • CustomerService – Handles profiles, preferences
  • LoyaltyEngine – Points, tiers, reward redemption
  • ReferralService – Invites and crediting
  • MarketingService – Segmentation and campaigns
  • CouponEngine – One-time codes, limits, expiry
[Frontend / POS / App] ─▶ [CustomerService] ─▶ [CustomerDB]
                               │       │
                               ▼       ▼
                     [LoyaltyEngine]  [ReferralService]
                               │       │
                               ▼       ▼
                    [CouponEngine]  [MarketingService]

docs.title: Loyalty Points Workflow:
docs.text:
1. Customer makes a purchase
  2. Points are calculated and stored
  3. Points contribute to tier level
  4. Redemption possible for eligible offers

docs.title: Referral Flow:
docs.text:
1. Customer shares referral code
  2. Friend registers & makes first purchase
  3. Both users are rewarded (points or coupon)

docs.title: Customers
docs.text:
• POST /customers – new profile
  • GET /customers/:id – fetch profile
  • PUT /customers/:id – update info

docs.title: Loyalty
docs.text:
• GET /loyalty/:id – view points & tier
  • POST /loyalty/redeem – use points

docs.title: Referrals
docs.text:
• POST /referral – generate code
  • POST /referral/claim – apply referral

docs.title: Coupons
docs.text:
• POST /coupon – create coupon
  • POST /coupon/redeem – use coupon

docs.title: Table: customers
docs.text: | id | name | email | phone | preferences (jsonb) |

docs.title: Table: loyalty
docs.text: | id | customer_id | points | tier | last_updated |

docs.title: Table: referrals
docs.text: | id | code | inviter_id | invitee_id | redeemed |

docs.title: Table: coupons
docs.text: | id | code | discount | expires_at | usage_limit | is_active |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Initial Code Stub
docs.text:
// loyalty.service.ts
app.post("/loyalty/redeem", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  const { customerId, points } = req.body;
  const success = await loyaltyEngine.redeem(customerId, points);
  res.status(success ? 200 : 400).json({ success });
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Use jsonb for storing flexible customer preferences
  • Tier logic should be configurable (admin panel rules)
  • Referral tracking must prevent abuse (limit redeems, cooldowns)
  • Coupons should support various types: % off, \$ off, free item

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
This module powers guest engagement and lifetime value through well-designed loyalty mechanics and reward strategies. It directly connects to marketing tools and customer satisfaction loops.

➡️ Next up: Analytics & Reporting

docs.title: 🌐 Purpose
docs.text: This document presents a high-level view of AuraConnect’s complete architecture. It ties together all core and optional modules in a cohesive ecosystem that supports multi-tenant restaurant operations with agentic AI, mobile support, and regulatory compliance.

docs.title: 📊 Global System Diagram
docs.text: (Ensure the image is added to docs/assets/global_architecture_map.png in the repo)

docs.title: 1. Client Interfaces
docs.text:
• Web Dashboard (React)
  • Mobile App (React Native with Offline Sync)
  • POS Terminal UI (for integrated POS)

docs.title: 2. Core Application Layer
docs.text: Module Description Staff Management Roles, shifts, access control Menu & Inventory Menu CRUD, inventory tracking, modifiers Order Management Front-of-house + back-of-house coordination Customer & Loyalty CRM, loyalty points, feedback engine Analytics & Reporting KPIs, dashboards, smart insights Taxing & Payroll Compliance, payslips, tax filing

docs.title: 3. Optional Integration Layer
docs.text: Module Functionality POS Integration Vendor APIs for order, payment sync White-Labeling Support Custom domain + themes per tenant Offline Sync for Mobile Local DB & queuing with auto-sync AI Customization Suite Recommendations, smart replies, insights Regulatory & Compliance GDPR, audit logs, labor/tax compliance

docs.title: 4. Shared Services
docs.text:
• AuthService: JWT, RBAC
  • NotificationService: Email/SMS/push
  • Scheduler: CRON/real-time event triggers
  • FileService: Images, documents, receipts
  • AuditLogger: Immutable tracking across modules

docs.title: 🗃️ Data Layer
docs.text:
• PostgreSQL (Supabase or hosted)
  • Redis (optional for queue/cache)
  • Local mobile SQLite for offline
  • Object Storage (S3/Supabase for assets)

docs.title: 🔄 DevOps & Deployment
docs.text:
• Hosted via Docker or Railway/Render
  • Netlify for wiki/docs site
  • GitHub Actions for CI/CD
  • Versioned APIs with OpenAPI + Postman workspace

docs.title: 🤖 AI Layer
docs.text:
• OpenAI / Gemini integrations via AICore
  • Prompt-based workflows stored per tenant
  • Custom-trained embeddings (future optional)

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
AuraConnect’s architecture is modular, multi-tenant, and developer-ready. It scales from single-location setups to franchise networks and supports both real-time and offline-first operations.

Refer to each module’s dedicated documentation in the wiki for detailed implementation.

docs.title: 1. 🍽️ Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module manages the restaurant’s menu offerings and kitchen inventory. It allows for real-time menu updates, stock-level tracking, ingredient-level costing, and inventory alerting.

Goals:

  • Centralized menu and modifier management
  • Sync menu with POS and ordering platforms
  • Real-time inventory tracking with usage logs
  • Auto restock suggestions and vendor integration

docs.title: 2. 📋 Features
docs.text:
• CRUD for menus, categories, and items
  • Menu modifiers (e.g., spice level, add-ons)
  • Ingredient-based recipe linkage
  • Inventory depletion per sale
  • Restock alerts and reorder logs

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services:

  • MenuService – Menu & modifier APIs
  • InventoryService – Stock, vendors, tracking
  • SyncService – POS/ordering platform sync
  • NotificationService – Restock alerts
  • DB – PostgreSQL or Supabase
[Frontend] ──▶ [MenuService] ──▶ [MenuDB]
                     │
                     ▼
              [InventoryService] ──▶ [InventoryDB]
                     │                    │
                     ▼                    ▼
              [NotificationService]  [SyncService]

docs.title: Inventory Usage Flow:
docs.text:
1. Customer places an order
  2. System maps order to ingredients
  3. InventoryService deducts quantities
  4. If below threshold → Notification triggers

docs.title: Menu Update Flow:
docs.text:
1. Admin updates item or pricing
  2. Change stored in MenuDB
  3. SyncService pushes to connected platforms

docs.title: Menu Management
docs.text:
• GET /menu – all items
  • POST /menu – create item
  • PUT /menu/:id – update item
  • DELETE /menu/:id

docs.title: Modifiers
docs.text:
• GET /modifiers
  • POST /modifiers

docs.title: Inventory
docs.text:
• GET /stock
  • POST /stock
  • PUT /stock/:id
  • GET /vendors
  • POST /vendors

docs.title: Table: menu_items
docs.text: | id | name | price | category | ingredients (jsonb) |

docs.title: Table: inventory
docs.text: | id | item_name | quantity | unit | threshold |

docs.title: Table: modifiers
docs.text: | id | name | type | values (jsonb) |

docs.title: Table: vendors
docs.text: | id | name | contact | items_supplied (jsonb) |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Initial Code Stub
docs.text:
// inventory.service.ts
app.post("/stock", authenticate, authorize("manager"), async (req, res) => {
  const item = await db.insert("inventory", req.body);
  res.status(201).json(item);
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Modifiers should be stored in a normalized form for flexible combinations
  • Use jsonb for dynamic ingredient lists
  • SyncService may use webhooks or polling depending on 3rd-party integrations
  • Alerts can integrate with email, SMS, or Slack

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
This module is the backbone for both operations and experience — linking customer-facing menus with kitchen and vendor logistics. It’s key to enabling real-time POS sync, accurate costing, and automated supply management.

➡️ Next up: Order Management (FOH + BOH)

docs.title: 1. 🌐 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module ensures AuraConnect mobile clients (React Native) can operate without internet connectivity and sync automatically once back online. It enables order taking, staff access, and limited reporting even when offline.

Goals:

  • Cache essential data locally
  • Enable offline-first order creation and shift tracking
  • Sync queued changes on reconnection
  • Handle conflicts, retries, and partial sync gracefully

docs.title: 2. 🔋 Offline Features Supported
docs.text:
• Order creation and updates (queue-based)
  • Local staff login (with limited access tokens)
  • Local shift logs and clock-ins
  • Menu and price caching
  • Partial reporting (based on local store)

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Mobile Components:

  • SyncManager – Queues offline mutations
  • LocalDB – Secure on-device SQLite/AsyncStorage store
  • ConnectionMonitor – Detects connectivity
  • ConflictResolver – Handles dual-source data issues

Backend Components:

  • SyncAPI – Accepts batched or delayed updates
  • DeltaTracker – Version control for records
[Mobile App] ─▶ [SyncManager] ─▶ [LocalDB] ←→ [UI]
                        │
                        ▼
             [ConnectionMonitor] → [SyncAPI] (when online)
                                      ▼
                             [DeltaTracker + DB]

docs.title: Offline Order Flow:
docs.text:
1. User creates an order
  2. SyncManager saves to LocalDB & queue
  3. UI updates optimistically
  4. When online, queued orders sent to SyncAPI
  5. Server applies and returns confirmation or conflict

docs.title: Shift Log Flow:
docs.text:
1. Staff checks in (offline)
  2. Entry stored locally
  3. On reconnection, batched updates pushed

docs.title: Sync
docs.text:
• POST /sync/orders – batch order sync
  • POST /sync/shifts – batch shift updates
  • POST /sync/menu – push cached delta (admin only)

docs.title: Conflict Resolution
docs.text: • POST /sync/conflict/resolve – manual or rule-based resolution

docs.title: Local Store (Mobile)
docs.text:
• orders_offline
  • staff_logs_offline
  • menu_cache

docs.title: Server Tables (same as main modules)
docs.text: • Accept offline deltas with source: mobile_offline

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Mobile Code Stub
docs.text:
// syncManager.ts (React Native)
const queue = [];
function saveOffline(order) {
  queue.push(order);
  AsyncStorage.setItem("offlineQueue", JSON.stringify(queue));
}

function trySync() {
  if (isOnline()) {
    const queued = JSON.parse(AsyncStorage.getItem("offlineQueue"));
    sendToSyncAPI(queued);
  }
}

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Use React Native libraries like NetInfo, AsyncStorage, SQLite
  • Conflict resolution rules must be clear (e.g., last-write-wins vs manual merge)
  • Ensure secure storage of sensitive data (e.g., tokens, order info)
  • Retry mechanism with exponential backoff recommended

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
Offline Sync enables AuraConnect to function in unreliable network environments — critical for field operations, delivery zones, and rural venues. A robust sync system ensures a resilient user experience.

➡️ Final optional module coming up: AI Customization Suite

docs.title: 1. 🧾 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module facilitates seamless order flow from Front of House (FOH) to Back of House (BOH). It ensures real-time updates, status tracking, and task routing between customers, servers, kitchen staff, and delivery.

Goals:

  • Enable order capture across dine-in, takeout, delivery
  • Sync FOH terminals with kitchen displays
  • Track order lifecycle: pending → in kitchen → ready → served
  • Route tasks based on roles and stations (e.g. grill, fry, bar)

docs.title: 2. 📋 Features
docs.text:
• Multi-source order intake (POS, kiosk, QR, mobile)
  • Status pipeline with timestamps
  • Item routing to respective kitchen stations
  • Real-time updates for FOH staff
  • Delayed prep alerts and reminders

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services:

  • OrderService – Captures and tracks orders
  • RoutingEngine – Distributes order items to stations
  • KitchenDisplayService – Push updates to BOH UI
  • NotificationService – Delays, readiness pings
  • AuditLogger – For compliance & replay
[Customer UI / POS] ──▶ [OrderService] ──▶ [OrderDB]
                                │
                                ▼
                      [RoutingEngine] ──▶ [KitchenDisplayService]
                                │                 ▲
                                ▼                 │
                     [NotificationService]  [BOH Staff Interface]

docs.title: Order Lifecycle:
docs.text:
1. Order received (QR/app/POS)
  2. Items parsed and assigned to stations
  3. BOH sees item queue on their KDS (Kitchen Display System)
  4. Staff marks item as “In Progress” → “Ready”
  5. FOH notified → Delivered → Marked complete

docs.title: Order APIs
docs.text:
• POST /orders – create order
  • GET /orders – list all
  • GET /orders/:id – fetch order
  • PUT /orders/:id/status – update status

docs.title: Routing & Status
docs.text:
• POST /route – reroute item to station
  • GET /stations/:id/queue – current kitchen queue

docs.title: Table: orders
docs.text: | id | table_no | customer_id | status | created_at |

docs.title: Table: order_items
docs.text: | id | order_id | item_name | station | status | started_at | completed_at |

docs.title: Table: stations
docs.text: | id | name | staff_id |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Code Stub
docs.text:
// order.service.ts
app.post("/orders", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  const order = await db.insert("orders", req.body);
  routeItemsToStations(order);
  res.status(201).json(order);
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• WebSockets recommended for real-time updates to BOH and FOH
  • RoutingEngine logic must support custom rules (e.g. vegetarian-only stations)
  • Audit logs help with tracing issues and delays
  • Design for performance under kitchen load (use Redis queues or batching)

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
This module connects the guest-facing experience with BOH execution. A high-performing order management system is critical to fast service, reduced errors, and streamlined kitchen operations.

➡️ Next up: Customer & Loyalty Module

docs.title: 1. 🔌 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module enables AuraConnect to integrate seamlessly with external POS systems (hardware and software), synchronizing menu items, orders, payments, and receipts across platforms.

Goals:

  • Push menu, modifiers, and pricing to POS terminals
  • Pull sales/orders from POS into Analytics & Reports
  • Sync payments and print receipts
  • Enable bidirectional real-time updates (if supported)

docs.title: 2. 🔄 Key Integration Targets
docs.text:
• Square POS
  • Toast POS
  • Clover
  • Vend
  • Custom vendor-specific APIs (via middleware adapters)

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services:

  • POSBridgeService – Handles all outbound/inbound sync
  • AdapterLayer – Vendor-specific implementations
  • SyncScheduler – Interval-based and event-driven triggers
  • AuditLogger – Logs sync attempts and failures
[Aura Backend] ◀▶ [POSBridgeService] ◀▶ [AdapterLayer (Square/Toast/...)]
                        │                      ▲
                        ▼                      │
               [Order Sync / Menu Sync]   [POS APIs / SDKs]

docs.title: Menu Push Flow:
docs.text:
1. Menu update occurs in AuraConnect
  2. Sync trigger initiated
  3. Adapter formats payload to vendor schema
  4. POST request sent to POS API → Success or Error logged

docs.title: Order Pull Flow:
docs.text:
1. SyncScheduler initiates fetch (polling or webhook)
  2. Orders retrieved from POS system
  3. Converted into Aura schema and stored
  4. Forwarded to Analytics module

docs.title: Integration Management
docs.text:
• POST /integrations/pos/connect – add/store credentials
  • GET /integrations/pos/status – test sync
  • POST /integrations/pos/sync/menu
  • POST /integrations/pos/sync/orders

docs.title: Table: pos_integrations
docs.text: | id | vendor | credentials (jsonb) | connected_on |

docs.title: Table: pos_sync_logs
docs.text: | id | type | status | message | synced_at |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Code Stub
docs.text:
// posbridge.service.ts
app.post("/integrations/pos/sync/menu", authenticate, authorize("admin"), async (req, res) => {
  const success = await posBridge.syncMenuToVendor("square");
  res.status(success ? 200 : 500).json({ success });
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Use queue system for retries (e.g. failed pushes to vendor APIs)
  • AdapterLayer should abstract out differences in payloads and endpoints
  • Always log sync outcomes for debugging and compliance
  • Credentials must be encrypted at rest

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
POS Integration is the key to cross-platform operability. This module provides extensibility and control over how AuraConnect communicates with real-world POS systems, ensuring data consistency and centralized reporting.

➡️ Next up: White-Labeling Support

docs.title: 1. 🏛️ Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module helps restaurants adhere to local, regional, and international compliance standards in food safety, labor laws, digital data regulations (like GDPR), taxation, and audit trails.

Goals: - Provide regulatory templates per country/state - Enable automated logging of sensitive actions - Ensure auditability of tax, staff, and order data - Support food safety and health code tracking - Offer exportable compliance reports

docs.title: 2. ✅ Key Compliance Areas
docs.text:
• Taxation: GST, VAT, TIN, electronic invoicing
  • Labor: Working hours, overtime, age restrictions
  • Data Privacy: GDPR, CCPA, user data management
  • Food Safety: Expiry tracking, allergen tagging
  • Audit Trails: Immutable logs of sensitive actions

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services: - ComplianceEngine – Orchestrates validation & policies - AuditLogger – Immutable record keeper - PolicyStore – Configurable rules per region - ExportService – CSV/PDF log export - NotificationService – Reminders for non-compliance

[Aura Modules] ─▶ [ComplianceEngine] ─▶ [PolicyStore]
                           │                   │
                           ▼                   ▼
                  [AuditLogger]        [NotificationService]
                           │
                           ▼
                   [ExportService → Reports]

docs.title: Audit Logging Flow:
docs.text:
1. A manager modifies a tax rule
  2. ComplianceEngine logs the action
  3. AuditLogger stores immutable log with metadata

docs.title: Policy Alert Flow:
docs.text:
1. Shift exceeds legal max duration
  2. NotificationService alerts admin

docs.title: Data Request Flow:
docs.text:
1. Customer requests data deletion (GDPR)
  2. System queues and completes anonymization

docs.title: Policy Management
docs.text:
• GET /compliance/policies/:region
  • POST /compliance/policies – add custom rule

docs.title: Audit Logs
docs.text: • GET /compliance/auditlogs – query by user/module/date

docs.title: Regulatory Exports
docs.text:
• GET /compliance/export/audit
  • GET /compliance/export/tax
  • GET /compliance/export/labor

docs.title: Table: audit_logs
docs.text: | id | action | module | user_id | meta (jsonb) | timestamp |

docs.title: Table: compliance_policies
docs.text: | id | region | type | rule_json | created_at |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Code Stub
docs.text:
// auditLogger.ts
function logAudit(action, module, userId, meta) {
  db.insert("audit_logs", { action, module, user_id: userId, meta, timestamp: new Date() });
}

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Use append-only design for audit_logs
  • Support versioned policies for future-proofing
  • Include legal text downloads for tax/HR compliance
  • Consider external auditor role with read-only access

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
This add-on ensures AuraConnect deployments can meet regulatory standards and pass audits confidently. It promotes responsible data use, staff safety, and tax/legal transparency.

✔️ All modules completed! AuraConnect is now fully architected.

docs.title: 1. 🎯 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
The Staff Management Module allows restaurants to efficiently onboard, manage, and monitor employees such as managers, chefs, servers, and delivery staff. It handles roles, shifts, permissions, profiles, and access control.

Goals:

  • Onboard staff with custom roles
  • Role-based access to AuraConnect modules
  • Manage shift schedules and leaves
  • Track performance and attendance
  • Ensure compliance and audit readiness

docs.title: Supported Roles (Configurable):
docs.text:
• Admin – Full access
  • Manager – Ops & team supervision
  • Chef / Kitchen Staff – Kitchen-only interface
  • Server / FOH Staff – Orders, customer service
  • Delivery Staff – Delivery schedule, order status

docs.title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):
docs.text:
• Defined in the DB and enforced in middleware
  • Access tokens embed role-based scopes

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Diagram
docs.text:
Core Components:

  • StaffService (REST API layer)
  • AuthService (with token & permission engine)
  • UserDB (PostgreSQL or Supabase)
  • Scheduler (manages shift tables)
  • NotificationService (reminders, alerts)
[Frontend] ──▶ [StaffService API] ──▶ [UserDB]
                     │                     │
                     ▼                     ▼
             [AuthService]          [Scheduler]
                     │
              [NotificationService]

docs.title: Onboarding Flow:
docs.text:
1. Admin creates staff profile
  2. Assigns role + shift schedule
  3. System sends welcome email + app link
  4. Staff logs in and updates profile

docs.title: Shift Scheduling Flow:
docs.text:
1. Manager creates weekly shift plan
  2. Scheduler validates overlaps
  3. Staff confirms availability
  4. Finalized schedule notifies staff

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
• POST /auth/login
  • POST /auth/register
  • POST /auth/logout

docs.title: Staff Management
docs.text:
• GET /staff – list all staff
  • GET /staff/:id – view profile
  • POST /staff – add staff
  • PUT /staff/:id – update profile
  • DELETE /staff/:id – remove staff

docs.title: Shift & Roles
docs.text:
• GET /shifts – view schedule
  • POST /shifts – create shifts
  • POST /roles – create custom roles
  • GET /roles – list all roles

docs.title: Table: users
docs.text: id name email role is_active created_at

docs.title: Table: roles
docs.text: | id | name | permissions (jsonb) |

docs.title: Table: shifts
docs.text: | id | user_id | start_time | end_time | status |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Initial Code Stub (Optional)
docs.text:
// staff.service.ts (Node + Express)
app.post("/staff", authenticate, authorize("admin"), async (req, res) => {
  const staff = await db.insert("users", req.body);
  sendWelcomeEmail(staff.email);
  res.status(201).json(staff);
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Middleware should enforce role-based access control (RBAC)
  • Use JWT for session tokens with embedded scopes
  • Scheduler should auto-adjust for public holidays (extendable logic)
  • Notifications can be handled via Firebase or a queue system

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
This module empowers restaurant operators to scale and secure their workforce operations with RBAC, shift management, and reliable onboarding. It’s critical for supporting access control across all other modules.

➡️ Next up: Menu & Inventory System (will be structured in a new focused chat)

docs.title: 1. 💼 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module automates tax calculations and staff payroll based on configured rules, logged work hours, role-based rates, and region-specific compliance. It integrates with government APIs, accounting tools, and banks/payroll providers.

Goals:

  • Automate tax calculation on orders and salaries
  • Track logged hours and overtime
  • Generate payslips and tax forms
  • Support multi-location tax zones and laws

docs.title: 2. 📋 Core Features
docs.text:
• Tax rule configuration by location and category
  • Real-time tax applied at checkout (order-level)
  • Hour tracking and pay-grade management
  • Payroll calculation engine with tax deduction
  • Payslip generation and report export (PDF)
  • Integration hooks for government API filing

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services:

  • TaxEngine – Order tax rules, region-based logic
  • PayrollEngine – Calculates net pay, bonuses, taxes
  • TimeTracker – Work hours, shifts, overtime
  • PayslipService – Generates PDF payslips and records
  • ComplianceGateway – Hooks to tax/GST/EPF APIs
[OrderService / StaffService] ─▶ [TaxEngine / TimeTracker]
                 │                          │
                 ▼                          ▼
          [PayrollEngine]         [ComplianceGateway]
                 │                          │
                 ▼                          ▼
         [PayslipService]              [Export/Reports]

docs.title: Payroll Cycle Flow:
docs.text:
1. Staff logs work hours (TimeTracker)
  2. PayrollEngine pulls hours + pay-grade + tax rules
  3. Net salary calculated with deductions
  4. PayslipService generates slip + report
  5. Optionally sends to bank/payroll API

docs.title: Tax Calculation Flow:
docs.text:
1. Customer places order
  2. Location-based rules fetched
  3. TaxEngine applies percentages to subtotal
  4. Totals sent back to OrderService

docs.title: Tax
docs.text:
• GET /tax/rules/:location
  • POST /tax/apply – returns computed tax on input

docs.title: Payroll
docs.text:
• GET /payroll/:staffId
  • POST /payroll/run – generate payroll for period
  • GET /payroll/history/:staffId

docs.title: Time Tracking
docs.text:
• POST /timelog/start
  • POST /timelog/stop
  • GET /timelog/:staffId

docs.title: Table: tax_rules
docs.text: | id | location | category | rate_percent |

docs.title: Table: time_logs
docs.text: | id | staff_id | shift_id | start_time | end_time |

docs.title: Table: payroll
docs.text: | id | staff_id | gross | deductions | net | period |

docs.title: Table: payslips
docs.text: | id | payroll_id | pdf_url | issued_at |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Code Stub
docs.text:
// payroll.service.ts
app.post("/payroll/run", authenticate, authorize("admin"), async (req, res) => {
  const results = await payrollEngine.generateForPeriod(req.body);
  res.status(200).json(results);
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Tax rules can be dynamic or geo-based (e.g. state GST, VAT)
  • Make TimeTracker extensible for biometrics or manual logs
  • Payroll reports should support month-end exports (Excel/PDF)
  • Ensure data security and audit trails for compliance

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
This module handles the financial and legal backbone of restaurant operations — ensuring accurate pay and compliant taxation. It’s tightly linked with order flow and staff activity.

➡️ Core modules complete! Next: POS Integration (Optional module 1)

docs.title: 1. 🧩 Overview & Goals
docs.text:
This module enables platform branding to be customized per client (restaurant group, franchise, or white-label partner). It supports themes, logos, color schemes, domains, and even branded mobile apps.

Goals:

  • Allow each tenant to brand the UI with their logo and color scheme
  • Enable custom domains and white-label subdomains
  • Support theming overrides for web & mobile clients
  • Simplify branding configuration via admin dashboard

docs.title: 2. 🖼️ Branding Elements
docs.text:
• Logo & Favicon
  • Color Palette (primary, secondary, background)
  • Font family
  • Domain (custom or subdomain)
  • Footer/contact details

docs.title: 3. 🧱 Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Core Services:

  • BrandingService – Stores and applies tenant branding config
  • ThemeEngine – Compiles and renders tenant-specific styles
  • DomainRouter – Maps incoming requests to tenant
  • MobilePackager – For branded mobile builds (optional CI toolchain)
[User Request] ─▶ [DomainRouter] ─▶ [Tenant ID]
                        │
                        ▼
               [BrandingService] ─▶ [ThemeEngine / UI Layer]

docs.title: UI Branding Load Flow:
docs.text:
1. User opens mybrand.auraconnect.ai
  2. DomainRouter maps to tenant ID
  3. BrandingService fetches config
  4. ThemeEngine renders theme tokens
  5. UI applies theme dynamically

docs.title: Admin Branding Setup Flow:
docs.text:
1. Admin opens Branding Settings
  2. Uploads logo, selects colors/fonts
  3. Clicks preview and confirms
  4. Config is saved and rendered immediately

docs.title: Branding Config
docs.text:
• GET /branding/:tenantId – fetch branding
  • POST /branding – update config

docs.title: Domain Mapping
docs.text:
• POST /branding/domain – link domain
  • GET /branding/domain/:tenantId

docs.title: Table: branding_configs
docs.text: | id | tenant_id | logo_url | primary_color | font | updated_at |

docs.title: Table: custom_domains
docs.text: | id | tenant_id | domain | verified | created_at |

docs.title: 7. 🛠️ Code Stub
docs.text:
// branding.service.ts
app.get("/branding/:tenantId", async (req, res) => {
  const config = await db.query("SELECT * FROM branding_configs WHERE tenant_id = $1", [req.params.tenantId]);
  res.json(config);
});

docs.title: 8. 📘 Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Consider caching themes in CDN for performance
  • Support fallbacks (e.g., default logo if none provided)
  • Ensure safe rendering of styles to avoid injection
  • Mobile packaging can be CI/CD-based with env config injection

docs.title: ✅ Summary
docs.text:
White-labeling makes AuraConnect adaptable to franchises and partners. This module adds a vital layer of customization, making each tenant’s experience feel fully branded.

➡️ Next up: Offline Sync for Mobile

docs.title: Feature Docs
docs.text: This section contains detailed documentation and architecture for each major module.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Central agent engine for all modules using LangChain or CrewAI architecture.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Context-aware prompt generation
  • Multi-agent orchestration
  • LLM-to-action pipelines

docs.title: Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Base: ai_agents/agent_core.py
  • Tooling: tools.py, prompt_factory.py

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Central logic for agentic automation across the platform.

docs.title: System Flow
docs.text:
• Agent initialized based on context (e.g., reservation, payroll)
  • Inputs gathered from DB + user
  • Prompt generated dynamically
  • LLM response interpreted
  • Action triggered (DB update, notification, suggestion)

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Agent Core Engine
  • LangChain/CrewAI Framework
  • Prompt Factory
  • Tool Integration

docs.title: Developer Notes
docs.text: Each component should follow service-layer design with unit test coverage.

docs.title: Summary

docs.title: Key Features

  • Agent memory per client

docs.title: Developer Notes

docs.title: Overview

  • Prompt Trainer

  • Agent: ai_agents/payroll_agent.py

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Manages GST/VAT tax rules per region, calculates item-level tax, and generates audit reports.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Dynamic tax rules per location/item
  • AI optimization for billing structures
  • Downloadable monthly tax reports

docs.title: Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Logic: backend/services/tax_service.py
  • Agent: ai_agents/tax_agent.py
  • Models: TaxRule, Invoice, TaxRecord

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Calculates and applies GST/VAT based on region, item, and service type.

docs.title: System Flow
docs.text:
• Order placed with taxable items
  • Tax rules applied from config
  • AI Agent optimizes tax efficiency
  • Tax breakdown shown in invoice
  • Monthly reports generated

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Tax Rule Engine
  • Invoice Builder
  • AI Tax Optimizer
  • Report Generator

docs.title: Developer Notes
docs.text: Each component should follow service-layer design with unit test coverage.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Allows branding customization per restaurant including logos, domains, themes, and headers.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Multi-tenant CSS theming
  • Logo and favicon injection
  • Custom email/SMS branding

docs.title: Developer Notes
docs.text:
• Themes: modules/white_label/themes/
  • Loader: branding_service.py

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Supports branding per restaurant (themes, logos, domains).

docs.title: System Flow
docs.text:
• Tenant config loaded on login
  • Custom theme/assets applied
  • Email/SMS headers customized
  • Analytics scoped to brand

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Theme Loader
  • Tenant Config Manager
  • Custom Asset Engine
  • Brand Router

docs.title: Developer Notes
docs.text: Each component should follow service-layer design with unit test coverage.
